imageTools.cropDesc: Free crop with aspect ratio lock
imageTools.cropHint: Adjust crop area and click Apply Crop
imageTools.resizeHint: Adjust dimensions and click Apply Resize
imageTools.rotateHint: Adjust angle and click Apply Rotate
imageTools.brightnessHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Brightness
imageTools.contrastHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Contrast
imageTools.compressHint: Adjust parameters and click Start Compress
imageTools.tip1: JPEG format gives the best compression
imageTools.tip3: Reducing max width also reduces file size
imageTools.cropFeatureDesc: Our online image crop tool supports free cropping and aspect ratio locking to help you quickly extract the desired area from any image. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers, ensuring your privacy.
imageTools.cropFeature1Desc: Drag to select any area with precise control over the crop region
imageTools.cropFeature2Desc: Preset ratios like 1:1, 16:9, 4:3 for one-click locking
imageTools.cropFeature3Desc: Cropped images retain original resolution with no quality loss
imageTools.cropStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to crop
imageTools.cropStep2: Drag to adjust the crop area on the image, or select a preset ratio
imageTools.cropStep3: Confirm the crop area and click "Apply Crop"
imageTools.cropStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.cropFaq1Q: Will the cropped image be uploaded to a server?
imageTools.cropFaq1A: No. All cropping operations are performed locally in your browser using Canvas API. Your image data never leaves your device.
imageTools.cropFaq2A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.cropFaq3Q: Will the cropped image become blurry?
imageTools.cropFaq3A: No. Cropping only extracts a portion of the image without any scaling or compression, so the cropped result maintains the original sharpness.
imageTools.compressFeatureDesc: Our online image compression tool helps you significantly reduce image file sizes while maintaining visual quality. Supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP output formats with adjustable quality and max width — ideal for web uploads, email attachments, and more.
imageTools.compressFeature1Desc: Output in JPEG, PNG, or WebP to meet different needs
imageTools.compressFeature3Desc: Powered by Canvas API — pure browser processing, no upload wait
imageTools.compressStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to compress
imageTools.compressStep2: Adjust the quality slider, output format, and max width
imageTools.compressStep3: Click "Start Compress" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.compressStep4: View the compression comparison and click "Download" to save
imageTools.compressFaq1A: Compression does reduce quality to some degree, but by adjusting the quality parameter, you can find a good balance. We recommend starting at 80% quality.
imageTools.compressFaq2Q: Why does WebP format compress better?
imageTools.compressFaq2A: WebP is a modern image format by Google that uses more advanced compression algorithms. At the same quality level, WebP files are typically 25-35% smaller than JPEG, making it ideal for web use.
imageTools.compressFaq3Q: Will the compressed image resolution change?
imageTools.compressFaq3A: If you adjust the max width parameter, the image width will scale accordingly with height adjusted proportionally. If you keep max width unchanged, only quality reduction is applied to compress file size.
imageTools.resizeHint: Adjust dimensions and click Apply Resize
imageTools.resizeFeatureDesc: Our online image resize tool supports custom dimensions and preset ratios to help you quickly adjust image size. Supports aspect ratio lock to prevent image distortion. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.resizeFeature1Desc: High-quality resize algorithm powered by Canvas API, maintaining image clarity
imageTools.resizeFeature2Desc: Automatically locks aspect ratio to prevent image stretching
imageTools.resizeFeature3Desc: Provides common ratios like 50%, 75%, 150%, 200% for quick resize
imageTools.resizeStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to resize
imageTools.resizeStep2: Enter target dimensions or select a preset ratio
imageTools.resizeStep3: Click "Apply Resize" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.resizeStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.resizeFaq1Q: Will the resized image become blurry?
imageTools.resizeFaq1A: Resizing may cause some quality loss, especially when enlarging images. We recommend keeping the resize ratio within 200% for best visual results.
imageTools.resizeFaq2Q: How to keep the image from distorting?
imageTools.resizeFaq2A: Aspect ratio is locked by default. When you modify the width, the height automatically adjusts proportionally. To set a custom aspect ratio, unlock it first and set width and height separately.
imageTools.resizeFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.rotateHint: Adjust angle and click Apply Rotate
imageTools.rotateFeatureDesc: Our online image rotation tool supports any angle rotation and horizontal/vertical flipping to help you quickly adjust image orientation. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.rotateFeature1Desc: Supports 0-360 degree rotation for precise control of image orientation
imageTools.rotateFeature2Desc: Horizontal and vertical flip for quick image mirroring
imageTools.rotateFeature3Desc: Preview rotation effects in real-time during adjustment
imageTools.rotateStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to rotate
imageTools.rotateStep2: Drag the slider or enter angle value to select rotation angle
imageTools.rotateStep3: Click "Apply Rotate" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.rotateStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.rotateFaq1Q: Will the rotated image become blurry?
imageTools.rotateFaq1A: Rotation itself does not cause quality loss. However, if the angle is not a multiple of 90 degrees, slight blur may occur due to pixel resampling, which is normal.
imageTools.rotateFaq2A: You can directly enter 90, 180, 270 degree values, or use the preset rotation buttons to quickly rotate 90 degrees.
imageTools.rotateFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.brightnessHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Brightness
imageTools.brightnessFeatureDesc: Our online brightness adjustment tool helps you quickly adjust image brightness levels. Supports real-time preview. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.brightnessFeature1Desc: Preview brightness effects in real-time while dragging the slider
imageTools.brightnessFeature2Desc: Supports 0-200% brightness range to meet different needs
imageTools.brightnessFeature3Desc: Reset to original brightness instantly if not satisfied
imageTools.brightnessStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to adjust
imageTools.brightnessStep2: Drag the brightness slider to adjust image brightness level
imageTools.brightnessStep3: Click "Apply Brightness" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.brightnessStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.brightnessFaq1Q: Will brightness adjustment affect image quality?
imageTools.brightnessFaq1A: Brightness adjustment changes pixel values. Excessive adjustment may cause detail loss. We recommend moderate adjustments to maintain natural appearance.
imageTools.brightnessFaq2A: Drag the brightness slider to 100% position, or click the "Reset" button to restore original brightness.
imageTools.brightnessFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.contrastHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Contrast
imageTools.contrastFeatureDesc: Our online contrast adjustment tool helps you enhance image depth and clarity. Supports real-time preview. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.

imageTools.contrastFeature2Desc: Supports 0-200% contrast range to meet different needs
imageTools.contrastFeature3Desc: Reset to original contrast instantly if not satisfied
imageTools.contrastStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to adjust
imageTools.contrastStep2: Drag the contrast slider to adjust image depth
imageTools.contrastStep3: Click "Apply Contrast" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.contrastStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.contrastFaq1Q: Will contrast adjustment affect image quality?
imageTools.contrastFaq1A: Contrast adjustment changes pixel values. Excessive adjustment may cause detail loss. We recommend moderate adjustments to maintain natural appearance.
imageTools.contrastFaq2A: Drag the contrast slider to 100% position, or click the "Reset" button to restore original contrast.
imageTools.contrastFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.saturationHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Saturation
imageTools.saturationFeatureDesc: Our online saturation adjustment tool helps you adjust color vibrancy to make images more vivid or soft. Supports real-time preview. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.saturationFeature1Desc: Preview saturation effects in real-time while dragging the slider
imageTools.saturationFeature2Desc: Supports 0-200% saturation range to meet different needs
imageTools.saturationFeature3Desc: Reset to original saturation instantly if not satisfied
imageTools.saturationStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to adjust
imageTools.saturationStep2: Drag the saturation slider to adjust color vibrancy
imageTools.saturationStep3: Click "Apply Saturation" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.saturationStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.saturationFaq1Q: Will saturation adjustment affect image quality?
imageTools.saturationFaq1A: Saturation adjustment changes color intensity. Excessive adjustment may cause color distortion. We recommend moderate adjustments to maintain natural appearance.
imageTools.saturationFaq2A: Drag the saturation slider to 100% position, or click the "Reset" button to restore original saturation.
imageTools.saturationFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.hueHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Hue
imageTools.hueFeatureDesc: Our online hue adjustment tool helps you adjust the overall color tone of images for different color styles. Supports real-time preview. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.hueFeature1Desc: Preview hue effects in real-time while dragging the slider
imageTools.hueFeature2Desc: Supports 0-360 degree hue angle to meet different needs
imageTools.hueFeature3Desc: Reset to original hue instantly if not satisfied
imageTools.hueStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to adjust
imageTools.hueStep2: Drag the hue slider to adjust overall color tone
imageTools.hueStep3: Click "Apply Hue" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.hueStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.hueFaq1Q: Will hue adjustment affect image quality?
imageTools.hueFaq1A: Hue adjustment changes color distribution. Excessive adjustment may cause color distortion. We recommend moderate adjustments to maintain natural appearance.
imageTools.hueFaq2A: Drag the hue slider to 0 degrees position, or click the "Reset" button to restore original hue.
imageTools.hueFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
imageTools.grayscaleHint: Adjust slider and click Apply Grayscale
imageTools.grayscaleFeatureDesc: Our online grayscale tool helps you convert color images to black and white for classic monochrome effects. Supports real-time preview. All operations are performed locally in your browser — no image is ever uploaded to our servers.
imageTools.grayscaleFeature1Desc: Preview grayscale effects in real-time while dragging the slider
imageTools.grayscaleFeature2Desc: Supports 0-100% grayscale range to meet different needs
imageTools.grayscaleFeature3Desc: Reset to original color instantly if not satisfied
imageTools.grayscaleStep1: Click "Choose Image" to upload the picture you want to convert
imageTools.grayscaleStep2: Drag the grayscale slider to adjust black & white intensity
imageTools.grayscaleStep3: Click "Apply Grayscale" and wait for processing to complete
imageTools.grayscaleStep4: Preview the result on the right, then click "Download" to save
imageTools.grayscaleFaq1Q: Will grayscale conversion affect image quality?
imageTools.grayscaleFaq1A: Grayscale conversion itself does not cause quality loss. It only converts color information to brightness information. The converted image maintains original resolution and sharpness.
imageTools.grayscaleFaq2A: Drag the grayscale slider to 0% position, or click the "Reset" button to restore original color.
imageTools.grayscaleFaq3A: All common image formats are supported, including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, and GIF. The output format is PNG to ensure the best quality.
